properties.name: Community Information
properties.location: between the townships of Kennedys Creek and Carlisle River
properties.webBody:
This Community Information message is being issued for between the townships of Kennedys Creek and Carlisle River. 
  • This Community Information replaces the Advice Message issued on 13 February 2026.
  • There is a bushfire at Carlisle River that is under control.
  • Smoke may be visible from nearby communities and roads. 
  • Firefighters remain on site to continue to extinguish any remaining hotspots.
  • Crews assisted by heavy machinery are  working on rehabilitating affected tracks and stabilising key areas of the fire ground.
There is no immediate threat to the community and no action is required.
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.

Unless the situation changes, this will be the last message issued for this fire.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.  
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
properties.text:

properties.name: Advice
properties.location: Trawool south of Goulburn Valley Hwy, east of Trawool Fire Station.
properties.webBody:
This Advice message is being issued for Trawool south of Goulburn Valley Hwy, east of Trawool Fire Station..
  • There is a bushfire south of the Goulburn Valley Hwy, Trawool that is not yet under control.
  • This Advice replaces the Watch and Act issued at 9:37am 22 February, 2026.
There is currently no threat to communities, but you should continue to stay informed and monitor conditions.
What you should do: 
  • Monitor weather conditions and warnings.
  • Review your fire survival plan. 
  • If you do not have a plan, decide what you will do if the situation changes.
  • Only call 000 only if you see flame or a large column of smoke.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Firefighters are patrolling this fire.
  • With rain predicted over the coming days, residents in bushfire affected areas are reminded to check the safety of their private drinking water and water tanks, as runoff after a fire can lead to contamination.  
For more information: click here

  • Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) or call 13 11 70 for road closures.
Goulburn Valley Highway remains open.

The following roads are closed:
  •  Willow Track south of the Goulburn Valley Highway 
  • Main Road between East Falls Road and 833 Fairview Road
This message was issued by Country Fire Authority.

The next update is expected by 23/02/2026 04:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#VicFires)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service on 1800 555 677 and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.action: Monitor Conditions As They Are Changing
properties.location: Knockwood and the area north of Gaffneys Creek
properties.webBody:
This Watch and Act message is being issued for Knockwood and the area north of Gaffneys Creek.
  • There is a bushfire at Mansfield-Woods Point Rd, Gaffneys Creek that is not yet under control.
  • The bushfire is travelling from Gaffneys Creek in a south-easterly direction along German Spur Track towards Woods Point. 
  • This Watch and Act replaces the Watch and Act issued at 9:10 am.
The situation can change at any time. You must monitor conditions and be ready to act.
What you should do: 
  • Monitor changes to the situation. Increased wind speed, change in wind direction, smoke or poor visibility can indicate the situation is changing. 
  • Enact your fire survival plan. 
  • If you do not have a plan, decide what you will do if the situation changes. 
  • If you have time, check your neighbours to see if they are monitoring conditions. 
  • Protect yourself and your family.
Leave if the situation changes or you feel unsafe. 
Remember to:
  • Take your pets, medications, mobile phone and charger. 
  • Many people have died trying to leave at the last minute during fires. 
  • Travel to the home of family or friends that are away from the warning area.
Impacts in your area:
  • The following road is closed: Mansfield - Woods point Road is closed from Kevington to Woods Point. 
  • Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) or call 13 11 70 for road closures.
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.

The next update is expected by 23/02/2026 05:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226 
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (#VicFires) 
  • ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • For information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Advice
properties.webBody:
This is a Final Flash Flood Warning for Wodonga and nearby areas.
  • The immediate threat of flash flooding has passed. 
  • Dangerous hazards including floodwater, mud, debris, damaged roads and fallen trees may still be present.
  • This Advice message replaces the Watch and Act issued at 22/02/2026 05:41 PM.
The threat of flooding is reduced. You can now return to normal activity, but be aware of any hazards that may still exist in the warning area.
What you should do: 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - do not enter floodwater. 
  • Find alternative travel routes if roads or underpasses are flooded. 
  • Be aware of driving hazards, such as mud, debris, damaged roads and fallen trees. 
If your home or business has been flooded: 
  • Check for building damage before you enter. 
  • Have all electrical and gas equipment professionally tested before use. 
  • Clean and dry out the building as soon as possible. 
Call Triple Zero (000) in a life threatening emergency. For emergency assistance from the SES call 132 500.
Impacts in your area:
  • For road closure information or report road hazards, call VicRoads 13 11 70, check the VicTraffic website (www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au), or contact your local council.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service. 

This will be the last warning issued for this flood. 

Flood information: 
  • Further flash flood information for some areas can be found in Local Flood Guides. 
  • For river heights check the Bureau of Meteorology website or phone 1300 659 217. 
Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226 
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods) 
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV. 
Accessibility: 
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service on 1800 555 677 and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

This Community Information message is being issued for between the townships of Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand. 
  • This Community Information replaces the message issued on 17 February 2026. 
  • There is a bushfire at Carlisle River that is under control.
  • Smoke may be visible from nearby communities and roads. 
  • Firefighters remain on site to continuing to extinguish hotspots. 
  • Crews and heavy machinery are working on rehabilitating affected tracks and stabilising key areas of the fire ground.
There is currently no threat to communities however you should stay informed of fire and weather conditions. 
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.webBody:
This Watch and Act message is being issued for the area surrounding Woods Point. 
  • The a bushfire that started at Mansfield-Woods Point Rd, Gaffneys Creek is not yet under control. 
  • The bushfire is travelling from Gaffneys Creek in a south-easterly direction along German Spur Track towards Woods Point. 
  • Crews are using heavy equipment and aircraft to build containment lines and directly attack the fire. 
  • This Watch and Act replaces the Watch and Act issued at 4:47pm. 
The threat is reduced, but the situation can change at any time. You must monitor conditions and be ready to act.
What you should do:  
  • Monitor changes to the situation. Increased wind speed, change in wind direction, smoke or poor visibility can indicate the situation is changing. 
  • Enact your fire survival plan. 
  • If you do not have a plan, decide what you will do if the situation changes. 
  • If you have time, check your neighbours to see if they are monitoring conditions. 
  • Protect yourself and your family. 
Leave if the situation changes or you feel unsafe. Remember to: 
  • Take your pets, medications, mobile phone and charger. 
  • Many people have died trying to leave at the last minute during fires. 
  • Travel to the home of family or friends that are away from the warning area.
Impacts in your area: 
  • The following roads are closed: Mansfield - Woods point Road is closed from Kevington to Woods Point. 
  • Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) or call 13 11 70 for road closures.
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.   

The next update is expected by 23/02/2026 09:00 am or as the situation changes.   

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226 
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (#VicFires) 
  • ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.   
Accessibility: 
  • The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. For information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.location: the area surrounding A1 Mine Settlement
properties.webBody:
This Watch and Act message is being issued for the area surrounding A1 Mine Settlement. 
  •  It is Not Safe to Return to the area surrounding A1 Mine Settlement. 
  • There is a bushfire in the area surrounding A1 Mine Settlement that is not yet under control. 
  • The bushfire is travelling from Gaffneys Creek in a south-easterly direction along German Spur Track towards Woods Point.
  • This Watch and Act message replaces the Emergency Warning- Leave Immediately issued at 3:45 pm.
If you left he area surrounding A1 Mine Settlement, it is Not Safe to Return. Be aware of potential dangerous bushfire and tree hazards.

In the car: if you are travelling, do not enter the area. Make a u-turn and travel to safety. Slow down and turn on your headlights. Smoke will make it difficult to see. 
What you should do:

If you have not already left: 
  • Stay where you are and avoid dangerous bushfire and tree hazards.
  • Check you have enough food, drinking water and medications - if you do not, phone Triple Zero (000) for assistance.
If you have already left: 
  • Do not return to your home until a Safe to Return notification is issued.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
Impacts in your area:
  • The following road is closed: Mansfield - Woods point Road is closed from Kevington to Woods Point.
  • Check the VicTraffic website (traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) or call 13 11 70 for road closures.
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria. 

The next update is expected by 23/02/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes. 

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (#VicFires)
  • ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV. 
Accessibility: 
  • The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. To access this information in other languages, call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline. 
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
